en.extract:
The Sulawesi myzomela (Myzomela chloroptera) is a species of bird in the family Meliphagidae. It is endemic to Indonesia where it occurs in Sulawesi, Taliabu, Selayar and Tanah Jampea. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests, subtropical or tropical mangrove forests, and subtropical or tropical moist montane forests.

The '''Sulawesi myzomela''' ('''''Myzomela chloroptera''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Meliphagidae]]. It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to [[Indonesia]] where it occurs in [[Sulawesi]], [[Taliabu Island Regency|Taliabu]], [[Selayar Island|Selayar]] and [[Tanah Jampea]]. Its natural [[habitat]]s are subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s, subtropical or tropical mangrove forests, and subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]s.
